context.setting: Working on laptop with laptop on Left

episode_text:
day=9; meal=chicken nuggets, broccoli, and ketchup; setting=Working on laptop with laptop on Left; time_of_day=noon;
preferences: microwave_time=3 min; occlusion_relevance=minimize left occlusion; robot_speed=slow; skewering_axis=perpendicular to major axis; web_interface_confirmation=no; transfer_mode=inside mouth transfer; outside_mouth_distance=not applicable; convey_robot_ready_for_initiating_transfer=speech + LED; detect_user_ready_for_initiating_transfer_feeding=open mouth; detect_user_ready_for_initiating_transfer_drinking=open mouth; detect_user_ready_for_initiating_transfer_wiping=autocontinue; convey_robot_ready_for_completing_transfer=speech + LED; detect_user_completed_transfer_feeding=perception; detect_user_completed_transfer_drinking=perception; detect_user_completed_transfer_wiping=perception; retract_between_bites=no; bite_dipping_preference=do not dip; wait_before_autocontinue_seconds=100 sec

microwave_time.user_tendencies: IF quiet personal setting AND neutral affect AND fruit meal or other foods intended to be served cold THEN no microwave BECAUSE observed cold-served fruit meals used no reheating. IF quiet personal setting AND neutral affect AND foods without sauces or dips that are not cold-served fruit or dessert THEN 1 min BECAUSE an earlier personal savory meal used light reheating. IF working context AND neutral affect AND warm savory foods such as bite-sized sandwiches, chicken nuggets, or broccoli THEN 2 min BECAUSE multiple working meals selected stronger reheating for warm savory foods. IF working context AND neutral affect AND warm savory foods such as chicken nuggets and broccoli in a left-laptop setup THEN 3 min BECAUSE the new left-side working meal selected the hottest reheating, indicating some working meals favor maximum warmth. IF social setting AND neutral affect AND warm savory foods such as chicken nuggets and vegetables THEN 2 min BECAUSE the observed social savory meal selected stronger reheating. IF social setting AND neutral affect AND fruit meal intended to be served cold THEN no microwave BECAUSE the observed social fruit meal also used no reheating. IF watching TV context AND neutral affect AND warm savory foods such as buffalo chicken bites, potato wedges, chicken nuggets, or broccoli THEN 3 min BECAUSE both TV meals selected the hottest reheating level. IF watching TV context AND neutral affect AND warm savory foods even when a sauce is present but not used for dipping THEN 3 min BECAUSE a TV meal with ketchup still used maximum reheating.

occlusion_relevance.user_tendencies: IF working on a laptop or tablet positioned on the left AND neutral affect AND any food condition THEN minimize left occlusion BECAUSE the new left-side working meal explicitly protected visibility of the device. IF working on a laptop or tablet positioned on the right AND neutral affect AND any food condition THEN minimize right occlusion BECAUSE one earlier right-side working meal explicitly protected visibility of the device. IF working on a laptop or tablet positioned on the right AND neutral affect AND warm savory foods such as chicken nuggets and broccoli THEN do not consider occlusion BECAUSE a later right-side working meal did not prioritize view protection, so right-side working contexts remain mixed. IF social setting with a person on the right AND neutral affect AND any food condition THEN minimize right occlusion BECAUSE the observed social meal prioritized keeping the right side visually open. IF social setting with a person on the left AND neutral affect AND any food condition THEN minimize left occlusion BECAUSE the earlier social meal prioritized keeping the left side visually open. IF watching TV in front AND neutral affect AND any food condition THEN minimize front occlusion BECAUSE the earlier TV meal explicitly protected the front view. IF watching TV with the TV on the right AND neutral affect AND any food condition THEN do not consider occlusion BECAUSE the TV-on-right meal did not prioritize view protection. IF quiet personal setting AND neutral affect AND any food condition THEN do not consider occlusion BECAUSE the observed personal meal did not prioritize occlusion. Overall, occlusion concern is selective and usually tracks the location of a socially or visually important target, though right-side working contexts show inconsistent choices.

robot_speed.user_tendencies: IF quiet personal setting AND neutral affect AND foods without sauces or dips THEN slow BECAUSE the observed personal meal used a slower pace. IF social setting AND neutral affect AND warm savory foods or fruit meals THEN slow BECAUSE both social meals selected the slowest speed. IF watching TV in front AND neutral affect AND warm savory foods with dip available THEN slow BECAUSE the earlier TV meal used the slowest speed. IF watching TV with the TV on the right AND neutral affect AND warm savory foods such as chicken nuggets and broccoli THEN medium BECAUSE the TV-on-right meal increased speed relative to other leisure contexts. IF working context AND neutral affect AND strong efficiency pressure is present THEN fast BECAUSE one working meal favored the fastest option. IF working context AND neutral affect AND warm savory foods such as chicken nuggets, broccoli, or bite-sized sandwiches THEN medium BECAUSE multiple working meals selected a moderate speed. IF working context AND neutral affect AND laptop on the left AND warm savory foods such as chicken nuggets and broccoli THEN slow BECAUSE the new left-side working meal matched the user's broader comfort-oriented pace rather than efficiency. Overall, the user is generally comfort-oriented in personal and social settings, and working contexts vary between efficiency and comfort rather than showing a single stable speed.

skewering_axis.user_tendencies: IF quiet personal setting AND neutral affect AND solid bite-sized foods in general THEN perpendicular to major axis BECAUSE observed personal meals used the reliability-oriented option. IF social setting AND neutral affect AND foods such as chicken nuggets, broccoli, or cut fruit THEN perpendicular to major axis BECAUSE both social meals selected the reliability-oriented option. IF watching TV context AND neutral affect AND foods such as buffalo chicken bites, potato wedges, chicken nuggets, or broccoli THEN perpendicular to major axis BECAUSE both TV meals also selected the reliability-oriented option. IF working context AND neutral affect AND warm savory foods such as chicken nuggets and broccoli THEN perpendicular to major axis BECAUSE working savory meals, including the new left-side working meal, matched the broader reliability-oriented pattern. IF working context AND neutral affect AND bite-sized sandwiches THEN parallel to major axis BECAUSE one working meal selected the narrower-bite option, suggesting food geometry can outweigh the broader preference for acquisition reliability.

web_interface_confirmation.user_tendencies: IF quiet personal setting AND neutral affect AND any food condition THEN no BECAUSE observed personal meals avoided extra confirmation, consistent with minimizing interaction burden. IF working context AND neutral affect AND any food condition THEN no BECAUSE working meals also skipped confirmation while occupied. IF social setting AND neutral affect AND any food condition THEN no BECAUSE social meals avoided extra confirmation. IF watching TV context AND neutral affect AND any food condition THEN no BECAUSE TV meals also skipped confirmation. No context-dependent variation observed yet.

transfer_mode.user_tendencies: IF any dining context AND any affective state AND any food condition THEN inside mouth transfer BECAUSE the user cannot lean forward due to lack of trunk control, making outside-mouth transfer physically unsuitable.

outside_mouth_distance.user_tendencies: IF any dining context AND any affective state AND any food condition THEN not applicable BECAUSE the user's physical abilities support inside mouth transfer rather than outside-mouth transfer.

convey_robot_ready_for_initiating_transf.user_tendencies: IF quiet personal setting AND neutral affect AND any food condition THEN speech + LED BECAUSE the observed personal meal used multimodal signaling. IF social setting AND neutral affect AND any food condition THEN speech + LED BECAUSE social meals used multimodal signaling, suggesting a preference for salient cues when another person is present. IF watching TV context AND neutral affect AND any food condition THEN speech + LED BECAUSE TV meals used multimodal signaling, consistent with wanting salient cues when attention is partly directed elsewhere. IF working context AND neutral affect AND laptop on the right AND some meals favor lower visual complexity THEN speech BECAUSE one right-side working meal used an auditory cue only. IF working context AND neutral affect AND laptop on the right AND warm savory foods such as chicken nuggets and broccoli THEN speech + LED BECAUSE a later right-side working meal used multimodal cueing instead. IF working context AND neutral affect AND laptop on the left THEN speech + LED BECAUSE the new left-side working meal used multimodal cueing. Overall, multimodal cueing is the general preference, with mixed evidence for some right-side working meals.

detect_user_ready_for_initiating_transfe.user_tendencies: IF quiet personal setting AND neutral affect AND any food condition THEN open mouth BECAUSE the user has good mouth control and selected this low-effort, user-driven method in a personal setting. IF social setting AND neutral affect AND any food condition THEN open mouth BECAUSE social meals used intentional mouth-based initiation despite conversational risk, indicating the user often prefers preserving direct timing control over full automation in that context. IF watching TV in front AND neutral affect AND any food condition THEN open mouth BECAUSE the earlier TV meal also used intentional mouth-based initiation. IF watching TV with the TV on the right AND neutral affect AND warm savory foods such as chicken nuggets and broccoli THEN autocontinue BECAUSE the TV-on-right meal shifted to automation, suggesting divided attention can outweigh explicit control in some TV contexts. IF working context AND neutral affect AND warm savory foods such as chicken nuggets and broccoli THEN open mouth BECAUSE working savory meals, including the new left-side working meal, used intentional user-driven initiation. IF working context AND neutral affect AND some meals favor stronger automation THEN autocontinue BECAUSE one earlier working meal favored reduced interaction burden. IF working context AND neutral affect AND bite-sized sandwiches THEN open mouth BECAUSE another working meal also selected intentional user-driven initiation. IF any context AND any affective state AND button input would be required THEN open mouth or autocontinue rather than button BECAUSE severe upper-limb paralysis prevents physical button use; observed selections also avoid button input.

IF quiet personal setting AND neutral affect AND any food condition THEN open mouth BECAUSE the observed personal meal used this low-effort but intentional readiness signal for drinking. IF social setting AND neutral affect AND any food condition THEN open mouth BECAUSE social meals used intentional mouth-based sip initiation. IF watching TV in front AND neutral affect AND any food condition THEN open mouth BECAUSE the earlier TV meal also used intentional mouth-based sip initiation. IF watching TV with the TV on the right AND neutral affect AND warm savory foods such as chicken nuggets and broccoli THEN autocontinue BECAUSE the TV-on-right meal shifted to automated sip initiation. IF working context AND neutral affect AND warm savory foods such as chicken nuggets and broccoli THEN open mouth BECAUSE working savory meals, including the new left-side working meal, used intentional user-driven sip initiation. IF working context AND neutral affect AND some meals favor stronger automation THEN autocontinue BECAUSE one earlier working meal favored faster automated flow. IF working context AND neutral affect AND bite-sized sandwiches THEN open mouth BECAUSE another working meal selected intentional user-driven sip initiation. IF any context AND any affective state AND button input would be required THEN open mouth or autocontinue rather than button BECAUSE severe upper-limb paralysis prevents physical button use; observed selections also avoid button input.

IF quiet personal setting AND neutral affect AND any food condition THEN autocontinue BECAUSE the observed personal meal used automation for wiping initiation. IF working context AND neutral affect AND any food condition THEN autocontinue BECAUSE working meals, including the new left-side working meal, also used automation. IF social setting AND neutral affect AND any food condition THEN autocontinue BECAUSE social meals did the same. IF watching TV context AND neutral affect AND any food condition THEN autocontinue BECAUSE TV meals also used automation. IF any context AND any affective state AND button input would be required THEN autocontinue rather than button BECAUSE severe upper-limb paralysis prevents physical button use. No context-dependent variation observed yet.

convey_robot_ready_for_completing_transf.user_tendencies: IF quiet personal setting AND neutral affect AND any food condition THEN speech + LED BECAUSE the observed personal meal used multimodal completion cues. IF social setting AND neutral affect AND any food condition THEN speech + LED BECAUSE social meals used multimodal completion cues. IF watching TV context AND neutral affect AND any food condition THEN speech + LED BECAUSE TV meals used multimodal completion cues, consistent with wanting salient signals when attention is partly elsewhere. IF working context AND neutral affect AND laptop on the right AND some meals favor lower visual complexity THEN speech BECAUSE one right-side working meal used auditory completion cueing only. IF working context AND neutral affect AND laptop on the right AND warm savory foods such as chicken nuggets and broccoli THEN speech + LED BECAUSE a later right-side working meal used multimodal completion cueing instead. IF working context AND neutral affect AND laptop on the left THEN speech + LED BECAUSE the new left-side working meal used multimodal completion cueing. Overall, multimodal completion cueing is the general preference, with mixed evidence for some right-side working meals.

detect_user_completed_transfer_feeding.user_tendencies: IF any dining context AND any affective state AND any food condition THEN perception BECAUSE the user consistently selected reliable sensor-based completion detection for feeding, and button input is incompatible with severe upper-limb paralysis.

detect_user_completed_transfer_drinking.user_tendencies: IF quiet personal setting AND neutral affect AND any food condition THEN perception BECAUSE the observed personal meal used perception-based drink completion and the user has good head control. IF social setting AND neutral affect AND any food condition THEN perception BECAUSE social meals used perception-based drink completion. IF watching TV in front AND neutral affect AND any food condition THEN perception BECAUSE the earlier TV meal also used perception-based drink completion. IF watching TV with the TV on the right AND neutral affect AND warm savory foods such as chicken nuggets and broccoli THEN autocontinue BECAUSE the TV-on-right meal shifted to automated completion. IF working context AND neutral affect AND warm savory foods such as chicken nuggets and broccoli THEN perception BECAUSE working savory meals, including the new left-side working meal, used perception-based drink completion. IF any context AND any affective state AND button input would be required THEN perception or autocontinue rather than button BECAUSE severe upper-limb paralysis prevents physical button use.

detect_user_completed_transfer_wiping.user_tendencies: IF quiet personal setting AND neutral affect AND any food condition THEN perception BECAUSE the observed personal meal used perception-based wiping completion and the user has good head control. IF working context AND neutral affect AND any food condition THEN perception BECAUSE working meals, including the new left-side working savory meal, used perception-based wiping completion. IF social setting AND neutral affect AND any food condition THEN perception BECAUSE social meals used perception-based wiping completion. IF watching TV in front AND neutral affect AND any food condition THEN perception BECAUSE the earlier TV meal also used perception-based wiping completion. IF watching TV with the TV on the right AND neutral affect AND warm savory foods such as chicken nuggets and broccoli THEN autocontinue BECAUSE the TV-on-right meal shifted to automated wiping completion. IF any context AND any affective state AND button input would be required THEN perception or autocontinue rather than button BECAUSE severe upper-limb paralysis prevents physical button use.

retract_between_bites.user_tendencies: IF quiet personal setting AND neutral affect AND any food condition THEN no BECAUSE the observed personal meal kept the robot in place between tasks to reduce meal time. IF working context AND neutral affect AND any food condition THEN no BECAUSE working meals, including the new left-side working meal, consistently prioritized efficiency over reducing visual obstruction. IF social setting AND neutral affect AND any food condition THEN no BECAUSE social meals still preferred not retracting despite side-specific occlusion concerns. IF watching TV in front AND neutral affect AND any food condition THEN yes BECAUSE the earlier TV meal selected retracting, consistent with protecting the user's front view of the screen between tasks. IF watching TV with the TV on the right AND neutral affect AND any food condition THEN no BECAUSE the TV-on-right meal did not use retracting. Current evidence suggests the user usually prioritizes shorter meal flow, but will accept extra motion when sustained obstruction of a front-facing screen matters.

bite_dipping_preference.user_tendencies: IF quiet personal setting AND neutral affect AND foods without dippable items or sauces THEN do not dip BECAUSE the observed personal fruit meal used no dipping. IF social setting AND neutral affect AND fruit meal or foods without dippable items or sauces THEN do not dip BECAUSE the observed social fruit meal also used no dipping. IF social setting AND neutral affect AND foods include sauces or dips such as ketchup THEN less BECAUSE the earlier social savory meal selected some dipping but reduced amount, consistent with allowing flavor while limiting mess in front of another person. IF working context AND neutral affect AND foods include sauces or dips such as ketchup THEN do not dip BECAUSE working meals with ketchup, including the new left-side working meal, avoided dipping, consistent with reducing mess while occupied. IF working context AND neutral affect AND foods without clear dipping use, such as bite-sized sandwiches, THEN do not dip BECAUSE another working meal also selected no dipping. IF watching TV context AND neutral affect AND foods include sauces or dips such as ranch dressing or ketchup THEN do not dip BECAUSE TV meals avoided dipping, suggesting the user often prefers cleaner, lower-mess operation when attention is divided.

wait_before_autocontinue_seconds.user_tendencies: IF quiet personal setting AND neutral affect AND any food condition THEN 100 sec BECAUSE the observed personal meal used a longer wait, suggesting the user wants more time to respond when not rushed by another task. IF social setting AND neutral affect AND any food condition THEN 100 sec BECAUSE social meals used the longer wait, consistent with allowing more time when attention may be shared. IF watching TV context AND neutral affect AND any food condition THEN 100 sec BECAUSE TV meals used the longer wait, consistent with allowing time when attention is partly on the screen. IF working context AND neutral affect AND any food condition THEN 10 sec BECAUSE several working meals used the shortest wait, consistent with efficiency preferences while occupied. IF working context AND neutral affect AND laptop on the left AND warm savory foods such as chicken nuggets and broccoli THEN 100 sec BECAUSE the new left-side working meal selected the longer wait, showing that working contexts can also favor more response time. Overall, wait-time preference is stable at 100 sec outside work, while working meals show a split between efficiency-oriented short waits and more deliberate long waits.
